task_name: Builder repo-index runtime artifact and generator
goal: Unblock the live Phase-A dry-run by providing the target repo index that builderScopeResolver already expects at runtime. The current default Builder target is soulmatch, so the committed builder/data/builder-repo-index.json must describe the soulmatch target paths, not Bluepilot's own builder/src tree. Add a deterministic generator/normalizer, commit the generated artifact, and prove resolveScope no longer fails because the index is missing.
scope_out: Do not modify scope-resolution, orchestrator, pipeline, gate, provider, smart-push, patch-mode, executor, or GitHub bridge logic.
Do not add network, LLM, provider, DB, or Maya calls to the index generator.
Do not open or touch any write kill-switch; real writes stay closed.
Do not change dependencies or package-lock.
Do not generate a Bluepilot-self index for this task; BP-137 defaults to the soulmatch target and needs soulmatch target paths.
claims: A new generator script can scan a supplied target source root recursively for TypeScript source files and writes builder/data/builder-repo-index.json.
The generated artifact has the full format consumed by builderScopeResolver and builderRelatedFiles: { totalFiles, files: [{ path, lines, exports }] }.
File paths are stable slash-separated paths relative to the target repo root, such as server/src/lib/opusTaskOrchestrator.ts, matching the soulmatch target profile used by orchestrateTask.
The export parser covers common exported function, const, class, interface, type, enum, async function, and named export forms without executing source code.

The package script makes the committed artifact reproducibly normalized and idempotent in a fresh Bluepilot checkout; the generator can fully regenerate from an explicit --source checkout when the target repo is available.
A test proves the committed artifact exists, matches the generator output, and allows resolveScope to run without throwing the missing-index error.
assumptions: Render runs bluepilot-builder with builder/ as Root Directory, so process.cwd()/data/builder-repo-index.json resolves to builder/data/builder-repo-index.json in the repo.
resolveScope consumes the index deterministically by text matching and does not require a live provider or Maya call.
The migrated orchestrator still defaults to targetProfileId soulmatch; therefore the index must contain soulmatch repo paths until a later task changes target selection explicitly.
BP-137 correctly exposes the dry-run trigger; BP-138 only supplies the runtime map needed before the dry-run chain can proceed.
If the live dry-run reveals a new provider, model, or later orchestration error after this task, that is progress beyond the missing-index blocker and belongs in a follow-up task.
dod: builder/data/builder-repo-index.json exists, is valid JSON, contains totalFiles plus files entries with path, lines, and exports, and includes known soulmatch paths such as server/src/lib/opusTaskOrchestrator.ts.
Running npm run index in builder/ normalizes the committed artifact; a second run leaves no git diff.
A test verifies the artifact against the generator output and verifies resolveScope does not throw builder-repo-index.json not found.
The generator source contains no fetch, http, https, process.env provider key reads, DB client usage, or Maya calls.
npm run typecheck in builder/ passes.
stop_conditions: The index cannot be generated from builder/src without modifying scope resolver logic.
A package-lock or dependency change becomes necessary.
A live network, provider, DB, Maya, or secret dependency becomes necessary.
Fixing a later dry-run failure would require changing orchestrator, provider, gate, or write-path logic.
prior_task_findings: BP-137 merged and deployed POST /probe/dry-run. The endpoint is live and accepts valid JSON, but the live Phase-A request returns dry_run_failed. Local reproduction showed the first blocker: builderScopeResolver throws 'builder-repo-index.json not found' before the dry-run chain can continue. The correct fix is to provide the index artifact and a reproducible generator, not to change the scope resolver.
